metadata.title:
WATER


POLLUTION

SPOKANE BOARD OF HEALTH


TO ASK REPUBLICAN CON-


vENTiON FOR ENDORSE
MENT OF SEWAGE LAWS.

pages.raw_text:
The influence of the republican
state convention which meets here
Thursday, will be sought by the
Spokane board OF health In a move
ment to prevent cities in this state
from dumping sewage into any
rivers. Resolutions to this effect
will be introduced with the object
of later having them Incorporated
into the statutes. If this succeeds
septic tanks of some of the other
processes will have to be devised to
take care of Spokane sewage.


The state board of health con-
templates action to prevent Occur
d'Alene from continuing to dump
sewage into the Spokane river on
the ground that it pollutes the
water supply Of Spokane. The ac-
tion will be a federal case based
on previous decisions that cities
have no right to pollute natural
water supplies.


The Spokane board of health has
no part in this action, as it has
been depending upon early compile
ton of the underground water sys-
tenn to remedy the danger without
the cost Of going to law.


Health Officer Grieve has been
informed by the city engineer that
the underground plant has been
completed with the exception of
connecting up water power to drive
the pumps. At the present stage
of water in the river it is tmpossi-
BILE to cut into the flume. A two
feet fall will be necessary to per-
NIT this part of the work being
done and then the plant will be
put into operation. 1t was expect
ed to have the underground supply
turned into the mains by June l,
but the date is indefinite on &C
count of the fume. Meanwhile the
river is absorbing the Occur
d'Alene sewage and Spokane Is alb
sorbing what it needs Of the river.
Physicians advise that all drinking
water be boiled until the new sup
ply is available.
